Patch Set 19: Code-Review-1

Mark it -1 based on previous reviews.

Any plan to refresh this patch?

Patch Set 19:

Yes but before I do which comments should I be looking at? I have been addressing several comments so far but most likely missed some.

Patch Set 19:

Hi James,

There are 39 style warnings in the following files:
 lnet/klnds/gnilnd/gnilnd.c
 lnet/klnds/o2iblnd/o2iblnd.c
 lnet/klnds/socklnd/socklnd.c
 lnet/utils/lnetconfig/liblnetconfig.c

Patch Set 19: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

I think that if we convert IP to hostname when show Nets, then we should allow parsing hostnames when we're configuring nets.

Also I have a comment regarding some CPT changes, that seem to not belong to this patch.

Patch Set 19:

For the CPT changes I assume you mean the socklnd changes. The ko2iblnd is just adding a field so we can report it back to user land.

Patch Set 19:

(1 comment)

I put the SMP changes into a separate patch. Will look into the net hostname output next.

Patch Set 22:

This change will never be back ported to 2.8 so it is less critical. I'm very flexible if you want to stomp all over it. The main goals here are:

1) Enable the same support in lnetctl as in lctl. Currently you can't even ping a   
    remote LNet interface with lnetctl :-( Mind the ping change is not in this  
    specific patch.

2) I like to see reported the CPT that a peer is bound too. That will influence the    
    work of the SMP scaling patch for Gemini LND that I plan to final finish off for   
    Lustre 2.9

Patch Set 23:

I am not a huge fan of the command set proposed here.  Here are some of the things that bother me:

I think the commands are backwards.  Rather than "add peer", it would be nice to copy the way that that ip command works, and make it "route add".

I don't like the inconsistency between "add peer"/"del peer", and "show peers".  "peer" vs "peers".  If use "list" as the command rather than "show", again following the ip command's example, that problem can go away.  The command can be "peer add", "peer del", "peer list".  Although I suppose "peer show" works too.

Patch Set 23:

Oh wait, maybe the order is already the way that I want.  The commit message might just be out of date.

parents.subject: LU-7101 lnet: per NI map-on-demand value

commit.subject: LU-5960 lnet: add in functionality of lctl to DLC
commit.message:
LU-5960 lnet: add in functionality of lctl to DLC

One of the barriers to moving from lctl to lnetctl
is the lack of certain functionality. This patch
adds some of the feature that are missing back.

The following commands have been added:
. show peers
. add peer
. del peer
. show connections
. close connection
. push connection

Besides adding in the missing pieces I have added
new features such as filtering the listing of peer
or connections based on properties such as NID,PID
or even LNet type (e.g o2ib1) to the lnetconfig
library. From the lnetctl command line one can
call lnetctl peer show --nid 10.0.0.1@tcp and it will
only show that specific peer. Listing of a grouping
of peers is also possible with for example lnetctl
peer show --nid o2ib12 which will display all peers
belong to the o2ib12 net configuration. Lastly the
ability to use DNS hostnames was also added.

Signed-off-by: Amir Shehata <amir.shehata@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Change-Id: Id102e8a6148d9e2ef8c88880df86e237a14c5ebb
